title: digitalis purpurea var. toletana
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. toletana, also known as Toletana foxglove, is a variant of the Digitalis purpurea plant, which is a biennial herb native to the Himalayan region and parts of India. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include a cardiac glycoside that helps to regulate heart rhythm and increase the force of heart contractions. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea var. toletana include treating conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, as well as reducing symptoms of edema and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Digitalis purpurea var. toletana are typically made by drying the leaves and flowers, which are then used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
